fact: Holds the all-time record for most goals in UEFA Champions League history with 140 goals.
Scored a record 17 goals in a single UEFA Champions League season (2013-14).
Has won 5 UEFA Champions League titles (1 with Manchester United, 4 with Real Madrid).
All-time international goal record holder with 143 goals in 226 appearances for Portugal.
First player to score in 5 different FIFA World Cups.
Has scored over 965 senior career goals for club and country as of early 2026.
Led Portugal to victory in the 2016 UEFA European Championship.
Scored 450 goals in 438 appearances for Real Madrid across all competitions.
Holds the record for most goals in a single calendar year by a European player in multiple seasons.
Won the UEFA Champions League with two different clubs (Manchester United and Real Madrid).
Scored 14 goals in UEFA European Championship history, the all-time record.
Has over 1,300 senior career appearances across club and country.
Scored 101 goals in 134 appearances during his time at Juventus.
Led Al-Nassr with high goal tallies in the Saudi Pro League since 2023.
Holds the record for most international appearances for Portugal (226 caps).
Won 3 Premier League titles with Manchester United.
Scored in every major competition he has played in across multiple clubs.

Demonstrated incredible longevity by continuing to score at elite levels past age 40.
Has won a record 8 Ballon d'Or awards.
Holds the record for most goals in La Liga history with 474 goals.

Scored over 32 goals in 75 appearances during his time at Paris Saint-Germain.
Known for record-breaking dribbling success (over 4,000 successful dribbles).
Led Inter Miami to major success including Leagues Cup and MLS Cup contributions.

Scored 101 goals in 134 appearances during his Juventus spell.

Scored in 5 different FIFA World Cups, a unique achievement.

Known for over 4,000 successful dribbles in his career.
Helped Inter Miami reach new heights including strong performances in the 2026 season.

Scored 183 career penalties — 70 more than Messi's 113. The undisputed Penalty King.
Scored only 113 career penalties despite fewer high-pressure attempts than Ronaldo.
Holds a higher penalty conversion rate of 83.6% compared to Messi's 77.9%.
Scored 4 penalties in the 2022 World Cup — still gets called 'Ponaldo' by fans worldwide.
Scored more long-range goals (outside the box) than Messi throughout his career.
Most of his goals came from inside the box with short build-up play.
Master of both feet — deadly accurate with powerful right-foot strikes from distance.
Primarily left-footed finisher. Relies heavily on his dominant left foot.
Scored numerous spectacular goals from 25-35+ yards with either foot.
Rarely scores from long distance compared to Ronaldo's thunderous strikes.
Scored 64 career free-kick goals using his signature knuckleball technique.
Has scored more free kicks than Ronaldo, but many from closer range.

Scored 129 goals in the UEFA Champions League throughout his career.
Scored more headers and powerful right-foot volleys from distance than any modern player.
Exceptional left-foot dribbling and finishing, but fewer aerial or long-range power goals.
Teams consistently trusted him with the most important penalties in knockout stages.
Missed more crucial penalties in big finals and knockout games than Ronaldo.
Scored goals from over 40 yards on multiple occasions — pure long-range menace.
Prefers close-range tap-ins and curled left-foot finishes inside the box.
Scored 12 direct free-kick goals in the Champions League — a record.
Scored several free kicks, but Ronaldo's were often from more difficult angles and distances.
Even at 41 years old in 2026, still scoring long-range screamers and penalties.
Reached his 900th career goal in 2026 — still 70 goals behind Ronaldo's total.
Superior right-foot finishing from outside the box and in the air.
Elite with his left foot, but significantly weaker with his right compared to Ronaldo.
Took and scored way more penalties under pressure across top European leagues.
Many of his penalties came in La Liga where Barcelona dominated possession.
Scored stunning long-distance free kicks and open-play goals from 30+ yards.

140 UCL goals including many from difficult angles and long range.
129 UCL goals — impressive, but Ronaldo has more in tougher knockout stages.
Better balance between left and right foot finishing than Messi.
Almost exclusively left-footed for his best work.

More goals scored from outside the penalty area across his entire career.
Exceptional inside the box, but Ronaldo dominates long-range finishing.
